Ella Fitzgerald: Just don't give up trying to do what you really want to do. Where there is love and inspiration, I don't think you can go wrong.
Billie Holiday: If I'm going to sing like someone else, then I don't need to sing at all.
Janis Joplin: Being an intellectual creates a lot of questions and no answers. You can fill your life up with ideas and still go home lonely. All you really have that matters is feelings.
Kurt Cobain: Wanting to be someone else is a waste of the person you are.
Amy Winehouse: I think it's very important to be yourself and not what other people want you to be.
Beyoncé Knowles: Your self-worth is determined by you. You don't have to depend on someone telling you who you are.
Taylor Swift: You are not the opinion of someone who doesn't know you.
Lady Gaga: Some women choose to follow men, and some choose to follow their dreams. If you're wondering which way to go, remember that your career will never wake up and tell you that it doesn't love you anymore.
Rihanna: You just have to have that fire in you. Nobody can give you that. Nobody can light it for you.
Ariana Grande: Be happy, be bright, be you.
Lin-Manuel Miranda: You are the only one who can write the story of your life. Write a good one.
Banksy: Art should comfort the disturbed and disturb the comfortable.
Ai Weiwei: You have to be a voice for the voiceless, and that's what I try to do.
Yayoi Kusama: With just one polka dot, you can change the shape of the world.
Marina Abramović: The function of the artist is to go beyond what is considered normal.
Ansel Adams: You don't take a photograph, you make it.
Dorothea Lange: The camera is an instrument that teaches people how to see without a camera.
Gordon Parks: I saw that the camera could be a weapon against poverty, against racism, against all sorts of wrongs.
James Nachtwey: I have been a witness, and these pictures are my testimony. The events I have recorded should not be forgotten and must not be repeated.
Diane Arbus: A photograph is a secret about a secret. The more it tells you, the less you know.
Marcel Proust: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.
James Thurber: Let us not look back in anger, nor forward in fear, but around in awareness.
Italo Calvino: The more enlightened our houses are, the more their walls ooze ghosts.
Zora Neale Hurston: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
Natalie Goldberg: Trust yourself. You’ve survived a lot, and you’ll survive whatever is coming.
